# Cold Handoff — `openclown-assistant` Orchestrator → New Devin Account

**Generated:** 2026-04-30 11:00 UTC, auto-cold after the close of the **TKT-005** (Onboarding Target Calculator) cycle.
**Type:** COLD (auto-generated, formal state only — no texture / no observations).
**Handoff target:** Fresh Devin session in any account that can be authenticated to the `OpenClown-bot/openclown-assistant` repo.
**Outgoing session:** `https://app.devin.ai/sessions/eb0d50df6f694d3e9c60f92a66e65dff` (continuation of warm-handoff session-2).

---

## 0. What you (new Devin) are looking at

You are taking over the **AI-engineering mentor / orchestrator** role for `openclown-assistant`. This file is your full bootstrap. Read every section before touching the repo.

Your three core obligations (full prompt in `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md`, read it after the self-checks in §1):

1. **Teach** the PO (non-engineer founder learning the SDLC).
2. **Push back** with evidence; cite artifacts and ADRs. Sycophancy is a failure mode.
3. **Protect docs-as-code:** every architectural decision must live in a versioned markdown file in git. "We agreed in chat" is not a decision.

You are NOT one of the four pipeline LLM agents (Business Planner, Architect, Executor, Reviewer). You are the conductor.

---

## 1. Self-check (run BEFORE asking the PO anything)

### 1a. `GITHUB_TOKEN_OPENCLOWN`

Call:

```
secrets action="list"
```

If you see `GITHUB_TOKEN_OPENCLOWN` (or fallback names like `GITHUB_PAT_TOKEN2`, `GITHUB_PAT`, `GH_TOKEN`) → OK, skip to 1b.

If none present, request via:

```
secrets action="request" secret_name="GITHUB_TOKEN_OPENCLOWN" type="plain" should_save=true save_scope="user" note="GitHub fine-grained PAT for OpenClown-bot/openclown-assistant: Contents R/W + Pull requests R/W + Workflows R/W"
```

Send a non-blocking message to the PO explaining what scopes the PAT needs.

### 1b. Repo clone

```bash
[[ -d ~/repos/openclown-assistant/.git ]] && echo "REPO_OK" || echo "REPO_MISSING"
```

If `REPO_OK` → `cd ~/repos/openclown-assistant && git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only origin main`.

If `REPO_MISSING`:

```bash
mkdir -p ~/repos
git clone https://git-manager.devin.ai/proxy/github.com/OpenClown-bot/openclown-assistant.git ~/repos/openclown-assistant
cd ~/repos/openclown-assistant
```

The native GitHub integration (Devin's git proxy) authenticates `git fetch` / `git push` directly without embedding the PAT in the URL.

### 1c. Validator green on current main

```bash
cd ~/repos/openclown-assistant
python3 scripts/validate_docs.py | tail -3
```

Expect: `validated 41 artifact(s); 0 failed` (after PR #36 merges; was 41/0 on the closure branch). If it fails, **stop and tell the PO** — main should never be in a broken validator state.

### 1d. `gh` CLI (Devin's native tools usually replace it)

```bash
gh --version 2>/dev/null && gh auth status 2>/dev/null && echo "GH_OK" || echo "GH_MISSING_OR_UNAUTHED"
```

In *this* outgoing session, `gh pr view` was disabled in the shell wrapper. Use the native tools wherever possible: `git_view_pr` / `git_create_pr` / `git_pr_checks` / `git_ci_job_logs` / `git_comment_on_pr` / `git_update_pr_description`. They are faster and always authenticated. The PAT auths git directly; you don't need `gh` for actual orchestrator work — only for read-only convenience like `gh pr view` (which `git_view_pr` already covers).

## 4. Pipeline (stable)

```
PRD (Business Planner)
  └→ Reviewer #1 (RV-SPEC, Kimi K2.6, ≠ Planner family)
       └→ ArchSpec + ADRs + Tickets (Architect)
            └→ Reviewer #2 (RV-SPEC, Kimi K2.6, ≠ Architect family)
                 └→ Code (Executor; one Ticket per session; GLM/Qwen/Codex)
                      └→ Reviewer #3 (RV-CODE, Kimi K2.6) + Devin Review bot
                           └→ PO merges
```

**Session independence.** Every artifact gets a new LLM session. No reuse across artifacts. **Exception:** Reviewer iter-N must reuse the same Kimi K2.6 opencode-session that produced iter-1 (continuity preserves context for re-eval).

**Branch hygiene for review PRs.** Reviewer sessions branch their review-PR **from `main`**, never from the artifact's own branch (per `docs/prompts/reviewer.md` §A.15 / §B.15).

**Q-TKT trigger.** If the spec lacks a decisive answer, Executor MUST create `docs/questions/Q-TKT-<NNN>-<NN>.md` and stop. Do NOT guess. This is codified in `docs/prompts/executor.md`; precedent: Q-TKT-005-01 produced ADR-005@0.2.0 amendment cycle.

**NUDGE iteration model.** When Reviewer / Devin Review surface findings, Executor (or Reviewer) addresses them in iter-N+1 on the same artifact/PR via continuation message into the existing opencode-session. Verdict scheme (`docs/prompts/reviewer.md` §A.14 / §B.14): `pass` (zero findings), `pass_with_changes` (zero HIGH findings; medium/low may remain deferred per PO), `fail` (≥1 HIGH finding).

---

## 5. Roles and write-zones (canonical: `CONTRIBUTING.md` lines 7-22)

| Role | Default model | Runtime | MAY write | MUST NOT write |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Product Owner (human) | — | — | Anything (final authority) | — |
| Business Planner | GPT-5.5 thinking / Opus 4.7 thinking | ChatGPT Plus / Devin | `docs/prd/` | Everything else |
| Architect | GPT-5.5 xhigh primary / Opus 4.7 thinking Windsurf alt | opencode CLI / Windsurf | `docs/architecture/`, `docs/tickets/` | `docs/prd/`, `src/`, `tests/`, `infra/`, repo root |
| Executor | GLM 5.1 default | opencode + OmniRoute | `src/`, `tests/`, append-only `docs/tickets/<id>.md#10 Execution Log`, ticket `status` (4 transitions only), `docs/questions/` | `docs/prd/`, `docs/architecture/`, any other ticket field, anything outside ticket §5 Outputs |
| Reviewer (LLM) | Kimi K2.6 | opencode + OmniRoute | `docs/reviews/` | Everything else; **NEVER `status: approved`** (PO-delegated orchestrator clerical patch sets that based on Reviewer verdict) |
| Reviewer (auto bot) | Devin Review | GitHub bot | inline PR comments | files in repo |
| **Orchestrator (you)** | Devin | webapp | Coordination + `docs/session-log/` + `docs/backlog/` (light) + ticket frontmatter promotions (`status`, `arch_ref`, `version`, `updated`, plus established closure fields `closed_at`/`closed_by`/`closure_pr`/`closure_commit`/`review_ref` per TKT-001..005 precedent) + RV frontmatter promotion (`status: in_review` → `approved` + `approved_at`/`approved_by`/`approved_after_iters`/`approved_note`) per `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md` §5 PO-delegated clerical patch carve-out | code, formal artifact bodies (PRD/ARCH/ADR/RV), substantive ticket body edits beyond reference-pinning, `docs/prompts/`, `CONTRIBUTING.md` |

**Orchestrator-write-zone hard rule (CONTRIBUTING.md line 22):** any orchestrator edit to files outside this declared write-zone (e.g. `CONTRIBUTING.md` itself, `docs/prompts/`, formal artifact bodies) requires **explicit PO authorisation recorded verbatim in the PR body**. Without it Reviewer / Devin Review will reject the PR.

### 6.4 Next step (for you, new orchestrator)

1. **Wait for PO to merge PR #36.** This is the last PR in the TKT-005 cycle. After merge, `main` advances and TKT-005 + RV-CODE-005 are formally `done`/`approved` on main.

2. **Open the auto-cold session-log PR** (this file). Once #36 is on main:
   ```bash
   cd ~/repos/openclown-assistant
   git checkout main && git pull --ff-only origin main
   git checkout -b devin/$(date +%s)-session-log-2026-04-30-session-1
   # Copy /tmp/2026-04-30-session-1.md → docs/session-log/2026-04-30-session-1.md
   git add docs/session-log/2026-04-30-session-1.md
   git commit -m "session-log: auto-cold after TKT-005 cycle close"
   git push -u origin HEAD
   ```
   Open via `git_create_pr`, title `session-log: auto-cold after TKT-005 cycle close`, base `main`. Auto-cold rule per `docs/session-log/README.md` §"Auto-cold rule".

3. **Address PO's response on the four TKT-NEW briefs.** PO will reply with one of: `promote A`, `promote B`, `defer C`, `route 1/2/3 для D`, etc. Per response:
   - **promote A/B/C** → orchestrator opens a draft TKT PR adding `docs/tickets/TKT-NNN-*.md` from `docs/tickets/TEMPLATE.md` (use next available TKT-016 / TKT-017 numbers; pin all refs `ID@X.Y.Z` per Hard rule 2).
   - **route 1 для D** → open ADR-NEW PR with Architect (new opencode-session, system prompt: `docs/prompts/architect.md`); Architect drafts ADR + cascades ARCH-001 if needed.
   - **route 3 для D** → orchestrator opens a `docs/backlog/` entry (light edit, in-write-zone) capturing the trade-off without ADR.
   - **defer X** → no action, capture in next session-log.

4. **(Side-quest, low-priority, carried from session-2 §6.4 step 7)** `architect.md` cleanup PR — refactor "External" references block: drop framework-specific model documentation references (Fireworks, OmniRoute, OpenRouter, GPT-5.5 announcement page); keep only `arena.ai`; add OpenClaw Plugins URLs (composio-community/awesome-openclaw-plugins, vincentkoc/awesome-openclaw, composio.dev/content/top-openclaw-plugins — PO emphasised this is the most important addition); add an instruction that when an LLM model selection is needed, Architect should NOT choose independently but surface a shortlist to PO via Q_TO_BUSINESS and wait for ratification. **Out-of-write-zone edit:** PR body MUST cite the PO's explicit verbal authorisation — see session-2 §7.2 for the verbatim quote. This is not a TKT-005 cycle dependency; it can run any time.

2. **Role-confirmation:** "I am the orchestrator. I will not write code, will not edit PRDs/ArchSpecs/ADRs/RVs (except clerical frontmatter promotions per `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md` §5). My write-zone is `docs/session-log/`, `docs/backlog/` (lightly), ticket frontmatter promotion fields, and RV frontmatter promotion fields. Other CONTRIBUTING.md / `docs/prompts/` / formal artifact body edits require explicit PO authorisation, recorded verbatim in the PR body."

4. **Wait for the PO to say "go"** before executing.

## 8. If something contradicts

If this file disagrees with `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md` or with `CONTRIBUTING.md` — **the repo files win, this snapshot loses**. Snapshots are volatile by definition; the repo is the source of truth across handoffs. Tell the PO what you found.
